summary: Strong Hand Jive & Jack Straw finale, solid Eyes of the World
blurb: The show delivers a lively Willie & The Hand Jive and an unexpected Jack Straw closer that showcase Jerry’s tight playing. The second set features a brisk Eyes Of The World, though the performance slips on Wharf Rat and Truckin'. Overall it’s an average 1986 outing with a few standout moments.
review: April 12, 1986 at Irvine Meadows finds the Dead in a generally solid mood. Jerry Garcia is praised for being “more cohesive and tight than the following night,” especially when he drives the upbeat Willie & The Hand Jive that kicks off the first set. The band takes an unexpected turn by closing the set with a spirited Jack Straw, a moment many listeners cite as a highlight of the evening. In the second set, Brent’s keyboards sit behind a surprisingly fast Eyes Of The World, giving the night a flash of 1980s energy, but the groove sputters on Wharf Rat and Truckin', where Bob Weir even forgets his lyrics and has to ask the crowd for help. The encore, The Mighty Quinn, rounds out an average show that’s worth hearing for its few bright spots despite some technical hiccups.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings label the sentiment as mixed, with positive notes on Jack Straw, Hand Jive and Eyes Of The World but negative mentions of Wharf Rat, Truckin' and lyrical slip‑ups. The balance leans slightly positive, yielding a modest +0.3 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good‑sounding audience tape that captures the lively Hand Jive and Jack Straw moments despite a few dropouts; it offers the most complete vibe of the show.

key_highlights: Unexpected Jack Straw closer with strong Jerry energy
Lively Willie & The Hand Jive that drives the first set
Fast, upbeat Eyes Of The World in the second set
Drop‑outs on Wharf Rat but still notable for its rarity

band_performance.Jerry: More cohesive and tight than the following night, delivering strong energy especially on Hand Jive.
band_performance.Bob: Forgets lyrics in Truckin' and asks the audience for help.
